events.title: New era for space dawns as Artemis astronauts return
events.summary: After a 10-day journey around the far side of the Moon, the four astronauts on NASA’s Artemis II mission are set to splash down in the Pacific Ocean late on Friday.

events.title: Sudan: 14 million displaced; hunger and attacks on health continue as war enters fourth year
events.summary: As Sudan approaches the third anniversary of a brutal civil war, millions remain displaced and hungry while the health system lies in ruins, with no end to the violence in sight, UN agencies said on Friday.

events.title: Lebanon airstrike casualties ‘still under the rubble’ as ambulances, hospitals face new threats
events.summary: With Lebanon still reeling from Israel’s devastating airstrikes on 8 April, UN humanitarians reported new fears of attacks on ambulances and looming food shortages in the south of the country on Friday.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 April: Fear and uncertainty grow in Lebanon as conflict grinds on
events.summary: Fighting continues across parts of the Middle East, with renewed exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon. Humanitarian needs are deepening, with rising civilian casualties, mounting displacement and growing strain on services. Di...

events.title: Kosovo mission head warns ‘mistrust’ threatens post-election stability
events.summary: Despite successful legislative elections in Kosovo late last year, a “delicate equilibrium” persists as deep divisions remain over the future of the United Nations presence in the region.

events.title: Lebanon: Health system overwhelmed following a ‘horrific’ day of Israeli strikes

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire
events.summary: With a US-Iran ceasefire offering a fragile glimmer of hope after weeks of conflict, violence continues to reverberate across the Middle East. Massive airstrikes in Lebanon have caused heavy civilian casualties and widespread destruction,...

events.title: Iran ceasefire raises hopes for reopening key Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: The announcement of a shaky two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran will, it is hoped, lead to the opening of the strategically important Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway through which one fifth of the world’s oil and gas passes.

events.title: Hundreds feared dead in Lebanon strikes
events.summary: The United Nations has strongly condemned airstrikes by the Israeli military across Lebanon on Wednesday which have resulted in significant casualties and destruction.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon

events.title: World News in Brief: Death on the Mediterranean, displacement in Afghanistan, ‘One Health’ summit
events.summary: Over 180 people are feared dead or missing in the latest shipwrecks on the Mediterranean, according to the UN migration agency, IOM.

events.title: Security Council: Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: ‘Entire families brutally erased’: Remembering the victims of the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi in Rwanda
events.summary: Thirty-two years ago, a genocidal campaign was unleashed against Rwanda’s Tutsi minority, resulting in more than one million deaths. On Tuesday, the UN is holding commemorations to ensure that the genocide is never forgotten and never repe...

events.title: World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security
events.summary: The UN is significantly scaling up its presence in the Sudanese capital, Khartoum, to expand life-saving operations as the conflict between rival militaries approaches its third year.
